entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The main crop studied, Pennisetum glaucum, grown in the West African Sahelian zone

entities.name: WEED GROWTH
entities.description: The growth and dry matter accumulation of weeds in the field

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of pearl millet measured in kg/ha

entities.name: LABOUR REQUIREMENTS
entities.description: The amount of human work required for weeding, measured in h/ha

entities.name: PRE-SOWING RIDGING
entities.description: A pre-sowing tillage practice used to manage soil and weeds

entities.name: HAND WEEDING
entities.description: Manual weed control method (Wf) providing full field weed removal

entities.name: ANIMAL TRACTION WEEDING
entities.description: Mechanical weeding powered by donkeys (Wa) targeting between-row weeds

entities.name: COMBINED WEEDING
entities.description: A combination of animal traction for between-row and hand weeding for within-row weeds (Wa+h)

entities.name: NO WEEDING
entities.description: A control method (W0) where no weed control is applied

entities.name: SANDY SOIL
entities.description: The soil type characteristic of the study area in Niger

entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SAHELIAN ZONE
entities.description: The broad geographic region where the research was conducted

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The specific country where the studies were conducted

entities.name: RAINFED FARMING
entities.description: Agricultural practice relying solely on rainfall for water

entities.name: WEEDS
entities.description: Unwanted plants that compete with pearl millet for resources

entities.name: DONKEYS
entities.description: Animals used to provide power for mechanical weeding

relationships.description: most effective at reducing weed growth
results in the highest crop yields
requires the highest amount of labour
reduces between-row weeds but allows within-row growth
reduces yield to 28-34% of hand weeding
results in yields 78-100% of hand weeding
requires less labour than full hand weeding
results in the highest weed dry matter
reduces yield to 2-10% of hand weeding
